channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Surrey Police Look Into Alleged Child Sexual Abuse Connected to Epstein Documents
item.description: The news article reportedly discussed an ongoing investigation by Surrey Police into allegations of child sexual abuse connected to documents associated with Jeffrey Epstein. Epstein, a financier who ...

item.title: Defendant in Ian Watkins murder trial denies harming baby he is accused of assaulting
item.description: In a murder trial at Leeds Crown Court, John McGowan-Fazakerley, aged 32, has given evidence denying that he ever harmed a baby he is accused of sexually assaulting. The case relates to the killing of...

item.title: Potential Impacts of Proposed Changes to U.S. Radiation Protection Standards
item.description: Recent developments in the United States have raised significant concerns regarding proposed changes to radiation protection standards. In May 2025, a Presidential Directive (EO 14300) instructed the ...

item.title: Hayden Panettiere Reveals Uncomfortable Encounters with Famous Men in New Memoir
item.description: In her new memoir, 'This Is Me: A Reckoning,' actress Hayden Panettiere recounts unsettling experiences with well-known men in the entertainment industry. At 19, she attended a party where a respected...

item.title: Norwegian journalist questions PM Modi on press freedom during Oslo visit, triggers online debate
item.description: During a joint press meet in Oslo with the Norwegian Prime Minister, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi did not take any questions from the media. As he was leaving the podium, Helle Lyng Svendsen, a...

item.title: First detection of H5N5 bird flu in a polar bear on Svalbard archipelago
item.description: Norwegian authorities have confirmed the detection of highly pathogenic avian influenza, commonly known as bird flu, in a dead polar bear on the Svalbard archipelago. This marks the first time the vir...

item.title: Contractual Disputes in Tanzanian Premier League: Main Causes and Examples
item.description: Contract disputes between players and clubs in Tanzania Mainland Premier League have become very common, showing deep problems in the way football is managed here. What used to be rare cases are now h...

item.title: Analysis of Rising Insecurity and Violence in Nigeria’s Sahel Region
item.description: Recent data from Acled and the Global Terrorism Index shows that after a brief period of improvement, insecurity in Nigeria has intensified, raising concerns as general elections approach. Experts att...

item.title: Jean Peters: The Private Star Who Shone Brightly in 1950s Hollywood
item.description: Jean Peters was a standout figure in 1950s Hollywood, celebrated for her natural beauty, versatility, and understated charm. Unlike many of her peers who actively sought public attention, Peters maint...

item.title: Zuchu starts masterclass to teach young Tanzanian artists how to succeed in music business
item.description: Tanzanian Bongo Flava star Zuchu has launched a special music masterclass called the Zuchu-Imbeju Masterclass 2026, aiming to equip upcoming singers, producers, managers, and behind-the-scenes creativ...

item.title: UK Release Date Announced for Canadian Comedy Film ‘Nirvanna The Band The Show The Movie’
item.description: The Canadian comedy film 'Nirvanna The Band The Show The Movie', adapted from the popular YouTube mockumentary and its Viceland TV spinoff, has finally secured a UK release. Directed by Matt Johnson a...

item.title: Life and Thoughts of Nalanda Commuters During a Bus Incident
item.description: This excerpt from Kalpish Ratna's 'Twice in Nalanda' narrates the inner lives of commuters from Nalanda Housing Society who face a tense situation while travelling in a battered minibus. The bus, once...

item.title: Police destroy P228 million worth of marijuana in Kalinga in two operations
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported that authorities have successfully destroyed around P228 million worth of marijuana plants in Kalinga during two eradication operations conducted on May 1...

item.title: Afterpay takes over naming rights of Sydney’s Qudos Bank Arena
item.description: Sydney's largest live entertainment venue, the 21,000-capacity Qudos Bank Arena in Homebush, is set for a rebrand. From July, the stadium, which attracts around 1.1 million visitors each year, will be...

item.title: Joburg Mayor Seeks Government Help to Avoid Citywide Power Cut Over R5.2bn Debt
item.description: Johannesburg mayor Dada Morero has urgently appealed to Electricity Minister Kgosientsho Ramokgopa to prevent the city from experiencing a widespread blackout due to an outstanding R5.2 billion debt o...

item.title: Kurt Cobain with Newborn Frances Bean at Home in West Hollywood, October 1992
item.description: In October 1992, photographer Stephen Sweet captured intimate photographs of Kurt Cobain at his home on Alta Loma Road in West Hollywood, California. The images show the Nirvana frontman holding his n...

item.title: Chinese Factory Owner Found Dead in Dar es Salaam, Police Investigating
item.description: Dar es Salaam has been shaken after the body of Bhaozang Ge, a 50-year-old Chinese businessman and owner of XIL LI plastic processing factory in Mabibo Sokoni, was found in a drainage trench within hi...

item.title: LPG crisis boosts adoption of biogas from wet waste, solar cookers and pellet stoves in India
item.description: The recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to West Asia conflict has caused a severe LPG shortage across India, leading to long queues, black marketing and prices skyrocketing up to Rs 5000 per cy...

item.title: Taifa Stars Drawn in Tough Group L for AFCON 2027 Qualifiers
item.description: Tanzania’s Taifa Stars have been drawn in Group L for the TotalEnergies CAF Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) PAMOJA 2027 qualifiers, facing strong opponents Nigeria, Madagascar, and Guinea-Bissau. Nigeri...

item.title: Met Police request reports from possible sexual assault victims linked to Married At First Sight UK
item.description: The Metropolitan Police have issued a public appeal for anyone who may have experienced sexual assault in connection with the television show 'Married At First Sight UK' to come forward and report the...
